front: When and how did World War II begin?
back: On 1 September 1939, Germany invaded Poland using Blitzkrieg. Two days later Britain and France declared war on Germany.
'Lightning war' — fast columns of tanks (Panzers) supported by aircraft that broke through and encircled the enemy quickly, letting Germany win without a long, economy-draining war of attrition.
After the German 'sickle cut' trapped the Allied armies, Operation Dynamo evacuated 338,000 British and French troops from the beaches — rescued by the navy and hundreds of civilian 'little ships.'

front: Why did France fall so fast in 1940?
back: Germany struck through the lightly defended Ardennes forest, bypassing the Maginot Line and trapping the best Allied armies. Paris fell and France surrendered just six weeks after the attack began.

front: What was the Battle of Britain, and why did Britain win?
back: Germany's 1940 air campaign to destroy the RAF before invading. Radar and the Spitfire and Hurricane let Britain fight smart; when Germany switched to bombing London, the RAF recovered and won, forcing Hitler to cancel the invasion.

Hitler's June 1941 invasion of the Soviet Union — the largest military operation in history. It aimed to destroy communism and seize 'living space,' but stalled in the vast distances and brutal Russian winter.

front: Why was Stalingrad the turning point?
back: In 1942–43 the Soviets encircled and destroyed an entire German army at Stalingrad. From then on Germany was in retreat in the East, and the myth of its invincibility was broken.

front: What was the significance of Pearl Harbor (Dec 1941)?
back: Japan's surprise attack on the U.S. fleet in Hawaii brought America — and its enormous industrial power — into the war, turning it into a truly global conflict.
The largest seaborne invasion in history, when the Western Allies landed in Normandy and opened a second front against Germany, leading to the liberation of Western Europe.

front: How did World War II end?
back: Germany surrendered in May 1945 after Soviet and Western armies converged and Hitler killed himself. Japan surrendered in August 1945 after the atomic bombing of Hiroshima and Nagasaki.
